---
title: "Availability and stock"
description: "How to mark an item out of stock, how timed returns work, and what diners see."
sidebarTitle: "Availability and stock"
icon: "circle-dot"
---

When a dish runs out, you don't delete it — you **mark it out of stock**.
Diners still see the item but it's visually dimmed and can't be added to
the cart. This page walks through the options.

## Where to change availability

Open any item in the **Items** tab of the menu editor. The **Availability**
picker is near the top of the dialog.

## The four modes

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="In stock">
    The default. The item is available and can be added to any cart.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Out until you change it">
    Indefinite out-of-stock. The item stays unavailable until someone on
    your team flips it back to **In stock**. Use this for discontinued
    items or long supply problems.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Out until tomorrow">
    The item is unavailable until the next local midnight in your venue's
    timezone. Use this when you're out for the rest of the day — the item
    comes back automatically at midnight without anyone having to
    remember.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Out until next opening">
    The item comes back at the start of your next
    [scheduled window](/vendors/menu/scheduling). Use this for lunch-only
    items that run out mid-service — they reappear the next time the menu
    opens instead of at midnight.
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

<Note>
**Out until next opening** only works if you've set up [open hours](/vendors/menu/scheduling)
for the menu. Without a schedule, Rumizi falls back to *tomorrow*.
</Note>

## Changes are instant

Unlike content edits (name, price, description), availability flips are
live immediately — you don't need to publish. A diner who reloads the
page sees the change.

## What diners see

An out-of-stock item:

- Still appears in the menu so diners understand the item exists.
- Is visually dimmed and labelled "Unavailable".
- Can't be added to the cart.
- Can't be suggested by the [menu assistant](/vendors/assistant/overview).

## Bringing an item back manually

Flip the picker back to **In stock**. Done. The next page load shows it.
